text:
We just raised our $15M Series A to scale our unified control plane for production AI.

AI is now mission-critical infrastructure, and we're building the reliability layer so it never breaks.

Thanks to our investors @ElevCap + @lightspeedvp

portkey.ai/blog/series-a-
media: image: Raises Series A $15M to Build AI That Never Breaks
notes: Pinned post establishes Portkey as production AI control plane and reliability layer.

text:
A great AI Gateway that stays niche is a nice tool.
A great AI Gateway that becomes ubiquitous changes how the industry works.
Today, @PortkeyAI has signed a definitive agreement to join @PaloAltoNtwks
notes: Founder/operator framing of acquisition as AI Gateway moving from niche to infrastructure.

text:
Exciting News: We've signed a definitive agreement to join Palo Alto Networks!

We started Portkey on a single idea: that AI in production would need a control plane. Not a feature, not a category somewhere on the side of the stack. Infrastructure. A place where governance,
media: link_card: paloaltonetworks.com Securing and Governing AI Agents At Scale Through A Unified AI Gateway
notes: High-signal company update. Text is partially hidden behind X show-more.

text:
We're excited to share our intent to acquire @PortkeyAI.

Once the acquisition has closed, we plan to integrate Portkey's AI Gateway into Prisma AIRS to establish a control plane to secure autonomous agents, thereby allowing companies to accelerate AI innovation with confidence.
notes: Acquirer post maps Portkey directly into Prisma AIRS and autonomous-agent security.
